from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Simple templates for common predicates
    templates = {
        "architecturalStyle": "{s} features {o} architectural style",
        "buildingStartDate": "{s} started construction in {o}",
        "completionDate": "{s} was completed in {o}",
        "floorCount": "{s} has {o} floors",
        "location": "{s} is located at {o}",
        "cost": "{s} cost {o}",
        "floorArea": "{s} has a floor area of {o} square metres",
        "architect": "{s} was designed by {o}",
        "owner": "{o} owns {s}",
        "formerName": "{s} was formerly known as {o}",
        "height": "{s} stands {o} metres tall",
        "buildingType": "{s} is a {o}",
        "developer": "{s} was developed by {o}",
        "tenant": "{s} is tenanted by {o}",
        "isPartOf": "{s} is part of {o}",
        "country": "{s} is in {o}",
        "currentTenants": "{s} currently houses {o}",
        "address": "{s} is addressed at {o}",
        "inaugurationDate": "{s} was inaugurated on {o}",
        "birthPlace": "{s} was born in {o}",
        "deathPlace": "{s} died in {o}",
        "bedCount": "{s} has {o} beds",
        "region": "{s} lies in the {o} region",
        "state": "{s} is located in {o}",
        "website": "{s} has a website at {o}",
        "yearOfConstruction": "{s} was constructed in {o}",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": (
            "{s} has the NRHP reference number {o}"
        ),
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": (
            "{s} was added to the National Register of Historic Places on {o}"
        ),
        "significantBuilding": "{s} is known for the significant building {o}",
        "governingBody": "{s} is governed by {o}",
        "leader": "The leader of {s} is {o}",
        "chancellor": "{s} has {o} as its chancellor",
        "governmentType": "{s} operates under a {o} government",
        "capital": "{s} has the capital {o}",
        "language": "{s} uses the {o}",
        "leaderTitle": "The leader title of {s} is {o}",
        "currency": "{s} uses the {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": "{s} includes the ethnic group {o}",
        "origin": "{s} originates from {o}",
        "significantProject": "{s} is notable for the project {o}",
        "foundationPlace": "{s} was founded in {o}",
        "demonym": "{s} is inhabited by {o}",
        "numberOfRooms": "{s} contains {o} rooms",
        "keyPerson": "{s} lists {o} as a key person",
        "architecture": "{s} showcases {o} architecture",
        "postalCode": "The postcode area of {s} is {o}",
    }

    clauses = []
    for triple in triples:
        tpl = templates.get(triple.predicate, "{s} {p} {o}")
        clause = tpl.format(s=triple.subject, p=triple.predicate, o=triple.object)
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Join clauses into a fluent sentence
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0]
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1]

    # Ensure proper punctuation
    if not sentence.endswith("."):
        sentence += "."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

metadata.changes:
Change 1: '"owner": "{s} is owned by {o}",' to '"owner": "{o} owns {s}",'
Change 2: '"leader": "{s} is led by {o}",' to '"leader": "The leader of {s} is {o}",'
Change 3: '"leaderTitle": "{s} has the leader title {o}",' to '"leaderTitle": "The leader title of {s} is {o}",'
Change 4: '"postalCode": "{s} has postal code {o}",' to '"postalCode": "The postcode area of {s} is {o}",'
